manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: mint, turkey, masher, yard, daffodil, bicycle, aunt, rosemary, onion.
Your opponent's team words are: kilometer, meter, goose, tractor, rose, badminton, nephew, oregano.
Distractor words are: tulip, peony, millimeter, grater, track, ostrich, father.
The kill words are: bowling.

message.content:
CLUE: cooking
TARGETS: turkey, masher, onion, rosemary, mint
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the words in any list (team, opponent, distractor, kill). Also cannot contain parts of them (like "turk" maybe part of turkey? Probably not allowed). Provide clue and list of target words.

We have team words: mint, turkey, masher, yard... etc.

We can try clue "herb" for mint, rosemary, oregano? But oregano is opponent. So can't include opponent words. So we must be careful not to accidentally point to opponent word. Clue "herb" would relate to mint, rosemary (our) and oregano (opponent) - that's dangerous. Could cause opponent guess. Probably not.

Clue "garden" might relate to daffodil, rosemary, mint, onion, yard maybe. Also rose is opponent, but rose is not garden? Could be ambiguous. Better avoid opponent words like rose. Garden may also point to rose (opponent) and maybe tulip (distractor). So risk.

Clue "bicycle" is a target word itself – cannot use that. Must be different.

Clue "spice" would relate to rosemary, mint, onion (maybe). Oregano opponent also. Also maybe turkey dish. Might be ambiguous.

Clue "spring" relates to daffodil (flower) and maybe yard. Not opponent. Could be fine.

Clue "yard" is target word itself, cannot.

Clue "bird" maybe goose (opponent) etc.

Clue "kitchen" could relate to masher, onion, rosemary, mint. Could also relate to grater (distractor). Might be OK. Opponent words less.

Clue "mashed" - not.

Better to choose a clue that only clearly points to a subset of our words, avoiding opponent/distractor.

Let's consider "garden" could be ambiguous but rose (opponent) and tulip (distractor). Might be too risky.

Clue "herbaceous" maybe weird.

Clue "spoon" relates to masher and maybe turkey? Not.

Clue "bush" maybe rosemary (shrub), mint (herb). Not opponent. Could be safe.

But need to list target words we think. We can give multiple.

Maybe choose clue "herbs" but can't because oregano is opponent. But maybe we only list mint, rosemary, maybe onion (not herb?). Onion not herb.

Better to give clue "cook" relating to masher, onion, turkey, maybe mint (as garnish). Could also imply nephew? Not.

But we must avoid opponent like badminton etc.

Clue "dinner" could be turkey, onion, rosemary, mint perhaps. Could also point to nephew? Not.

Probably safe to give clue "cooking" but that's not a single word? It's one word. It's ok. Not in lists.

Check: "cooking" not in any list. It may relate to masher, onion, turkey, rosemary, mint. No opponent/distractor.

Let's provide clue "cooking" and target list "turkey, masher, onion, rosemary, mint". Should be okay.

Make sure not to include a distractor like "yard"? Not.

Thus answer.
